message:
fix(security): block full 127.x.x.x loopback range and fix WebSocket race

url-security.ts:
- Add explicit check for 127.0.0.0/8 CIDR range (all 127.x.x.x addresses)
- Previous code only blocked 127.0.0.1 via localhost string match
- Attackers could bypass SSRF protection using 127.0.0.2, 127.1.1.1, etc.
- The entire 127.0.0.0/8 range (16M addresses) routes to loopback

url-security.test.ts:
- Add test cases for 127.0.0.2, 127.1.1.1, and 127.255.255.254
- Verifies the full loopback range is now blocked

websocket-context.tsx:
- Fix race condition where stale WebSocket callbacks could fire
- When reconnecting rapidly, old socket's onopen/onmessage/onclose/onerror
  could still fire after a new socket was created
- Added guards: if (wsRef.current !== ws) return early from all callbacks
- Moved wsRef.current = ws assignment AFTER event handler registration
  (ensures handlers can reference the correct socket instance)
- Prevents ghost messages and status flicker during reconnection

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
